user_query:
I'm reviewing a refinance and need a quick prototype to reconcile title data and draft a client update. Using the provided inputs under input/, please build and run a small command-line tool that extracts structured data, checks for inconsistencies, and produces three deliverables under output/.

Inputs to use:
- input/title_commitment.html (prelim title/commitment with Schedule A)
- input/deed_abstracts.jsonl (line-delimited deed abstracts in recording order)
- input/county_tax_roll.csv (current tax roll owner and situs)
- input/case.yaml (matter and client context for the email)

Please implement and run a script (e.g., tools/title_audit.py) that:
1) Extracts from the title commitment HTML at least: commitment number, effective date, APN/parcel, "Title Vested In" owner, legal description text, proposed insured, and property address.
2) Parses the deed abstracts JSONL into a timeline and builds a chain-of-title table (date, instrument, grantor, grantee, recording_reference, legal_snippet) in chronological order.
3) Compares across sources for this parcel: (a) owner name in title commitment vs latest grantee in deeds vs tax roll owner, (b) APN consistency (title commitment vs case.yaml vs tax roll), and (c) a basic chain continuity check where each deed’s grantor should match the prior deed’s grantee when the instrument transfers fee (you may include non-fee instruments in the timeline but they should not break continuity). Flag any mismatches or breaks.
4) Drafts a professional email to the client summarizing the findings, including the matter ID and property address in the subject, a brief narrative of the chain, a bullet list of discrepancies found (if any), and suggested next steps. Do not send the email; just produce the draft text.

Deliverables (must be written to these exact paths):
- output/chain_of_title.csv with header: record_date,instrument,grantor,grantee,recording_reference,legal_snippet
- output/discrepancy_report.json capturing at least: { "owner_name_comparison": { "title_commitment": ..., "deed_latest_grantee": ..., "tax_roll": ..., "match": true|false }, "apn_match": true|false, "legal_description_present": true|false, "chain_gaps": [ { "index": <int>, "prior_grantee": <string>, "current_grantor": <string>, "note": <string> } ] }
- output/draft_email_to_client.txt containing: a subject line starting with "Title Review - [matter_id]: [property_address]" and a clear body addressed to the client by name, summarizing results and referencing the two attached reports by filename

Run requirement: After implementing, actually run your script locally to generate the above files. Include a one-line command you used (e.g., "python tools/title_audit.py --input-commitment input/title_commitment.html --deeds input/deed_abstracts.jsonl --tax input/county_tax_roll.csv --case input/case.yaml --outdir output") in the first line of output/draft_email_to_client.txt below the subject so I can reproduce the run.

Notes and constraints:
- Keep the solution self-contained; do not call external services.
- The extraction from HTML/CSV/JSONL/YAML should be robust enough to pull the specified fields from these files.
- Owner name comparisons should be case- and punctuation-insensitive at minimum when determining a match; still list the raw values compared in the JSON report.
- Ensure all three deliverables are created under output/, and that discrepancies found in the JSON report are consistent with the inputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
